holes.content: Holistic judge without verification (no follow-up question answering): achieves F1 = 25.00 with precision 100.00%, recall 14.29%, accuracy 60.00% on GAIA-Web DRA verification task.
holes.label: alt_no_verification_verifier

holes.content:
DeepVerifier-8B (Qwen3-8B fine-tuned on DeepVerifier-4K + CK-Pro-8B data) achieves the highest accuracy among open-source models on GAIA-Full after 10 feedback rounds: 32.2% (up from 26.7% at 0 rounds, +5.5 points). This surpasses:
- CK-Pro-8B (CK-Pro training only): gains only 2.6 points with reflection
- Qwen3-8B (no reflection training): shows minimal improvement

Performance by GAIA difficulty level (GAIA-Full, after 10 rounds):

| Level | DeepVerifier-8B | CK-Pro-8B | Qwen3-8B |
|-------|----------------|-----------|----------|
| Level 1 | 40% | 38% | 36% |
| Level 2 | 32% | 29% | 28% |
| Level 3 | 11% | 8% | 8% |
| Average | 34% | 28% | 27% |
holes.label: deepverifier_8b_gaia

holes.content: Deep Research Agents (DRAs) remain prone to unreliable outputs in long-horizon tasks due to incorrect actions, API failures, hallucinations, and other errors. In tasks spanning dozens of pages and hundreds of actions, online human supervision is infeasible, severely constraining practical deployment.
holes.label: dra_unreliability

holes.content: The annotation process yields 555 error points across all incorrect trajectories in the WebAggregatorQA corpus.
holes.label: error_points_count

holes.content: The generation of structured, high-quality feedback for DRA outputs is a hard task requiring sophisticated reasoning capability. Reflexion-based methods that use raw textual feedback fail to reliably identify subtle reasoning failures, factual errors, or unsupported claims in long-horizon DRA trajectories.
holes.label: feedback_generation_is_hard

holes.content: Prior work on inference-time improvement for DRAs has largely emphasized scaling output tokens or selection across parallel rollouts (e.g., parallel sampling for optimal trajectory search, narrative-driven aggregation across iterations [@Zhu2025c]). None of these methods address systematic verification of DRA outputs or structured feedback generation for DRAs.
holes.label: test_time_scaling_gap

holes.content: The taxonomy construction corpus comprises 2,997 agent actions across 90 distinct tasks. Trajectory lengths range from 2 to 156 steps (average 33.3 steps). Token counts range from 18.7K to 60.0M tokens (average 8.2M tokens, total 738M tokens). The correct/incorrect ratio is 0.96 (nearly balanced).
holes.label: trajectory_corpus

holes.content:
Transition rates between consecutive feedback rounds on GAIA-Full (Claude-3.7-Sonnet):

| Round | Incorrect→Correct (%) | Correct→Incorrect (%) |
|-------|----------------------|----------------------|
| 1     | 18.99               | 12.79               |
| 2     | 9.33                | 4.44                |
| 3     | 6.94                | 4.30                |
| 4     | 8.45                | 1.06                |
| 5     | 0.00                | 3.03                |
| 6     | 1.45                | 0.00                |
| 7–10  | ≤1.45               | ≤1.03               |

The incorrect→correct rate is stronger but decays; the correct→incorrect rate is weaker but persists.
holes.label: transition_rates
